name: Crystal
summary: Statically-typed AOT-compiled language with Ruby-like syntax: require "foo" for textual-inclusion-style imports (idempotent via load-set), module Foo ... end declares hierarchical namespaces (Foo::Bar), Shards as the package manager.

sections.title: Overview
sections.content: Crystal (Ary Borenszweig and team, 2014+; v1.x stable) is a statically-typed AOT-compiled language with Ruby-inspired syntax. This record covers the language-level module-system surface.

sections.title: module Foo and require
sections.content:
Crystal uses `require "foo"` for textual inclusion-style imports, idempotent via a load-set. Modules are declared with `module Foo ... end` and form hierarchies (`Foo::Bar`). The combination is similar to Ruby's `require` plus Ruby's modules but with **static type checking and AOT compilation**.

**Shards** is the package manager. The language is small enough that the module system has not been a major design surface area; the distinctive work happens in the type inference (a global flow-sensitive type inference, not Hindley-Milner) and the LLVM-backed AOT compiler.
